recommendedNextStep: Run launch review against deployed preview and complete final compliance checks.

readyFoundations.title: Workspace verification and deployment artifacts
readyFoundations.summary: The repo already has an enforced verification path, CI execution, and exported topology artifacts for deployment handoff.
details: Local verification is codified in the root verify script.
CI runs the same verify path before exporting deployment artifacts.
Topology artifacts are published into docs and preview public assets for direct download.

evidence.note: Root verify script is defined.
CI runs the verify workflow.
Topology export publishes docs assets.
Topology export publishes preview assets.

readyFoundations.title: Worker analytics baseline
readyFoundations.summary: The auth and API workers now emit baseline request analytics for health, join, session, recording, moderation, and follow-up flows, which closes the previous zero-telemetry gap at the edge.
details: Both Workers now declare Cloudflare Analytics Engine bindings.
Shared helpers emit low-overhead route metrics with route, outcome, and client metadata.
This is a baseline telemetry layer; external alert routing is still separate launch work.

evidence.note: Auth worker declares an analytics dataset binding.
API worker declares an analytics dataset binding.
Auth worker emits Analytics Engine datapoints.
API worker emits Analytics Engine datapoints.

readyFoundations.title: Fail-closed auth and media guardrails
readyFoundations.summary: Mock auth is disabled by default in production config, and the media worker now fails closed when upload or download bases are not configured.
details: The auth worker no longer exposes the mock session path in production mode by default.
Media download and upload routes return explicit configuration errors instead of placeholder URLs.

evidence.note: Mock auth is disabled by default.
Download route fails closed when not configured.
Upload route fails closed when not configured.

readyFoundations.title: Media control-service boundary
readyFoundations.summary: The API worker now talks to the media worker over an explicit service binding, and the media worker proxies control requests to a dedicated backend only when configured.
details: Recording control no longer returns synthetic ids inside the adapter itself.
The media worker fails closed when no dedicated backend is configured.
This is the correct architectural boundary for a real media plane, even though the backend is still not live.

evidence.note: API worker now binds to the media worker.
Media worker exposes explicit control-plane routes.
Media control fails closed when no backend is configured.
Media adapter now calls the control boundary instead of generating ids locally.

readyFoundations.title: Persistence mode guardrails
readyFoundations.summary: If Postgres mode is selected before the durable adapter exists, the API now fails early with a clear 503 instead of leaking that mismatch through arbitrary route failures.
details: The persistence availability decision now lives in one shared helper.
The main router blocks non-health traffic before route execution when Postgres mode is unavailable.
Repository access uses the same guard, which keeps direct route-level data access aligned with the top-level worker behavior.

evidence.note: API exposes a shared persistence-availability helper.
Unavailable Postgres mode now maps to a clear 503 error code.
Repository access uses the shared persistence guard.
Top-level API routing blocks unavailable persistence mode before executing routes.

readyFoundations.title: Postgres runtime-state adapter
readyFoundations.summary: The DB package now has a real Postgres-backed persistence path that loads and commits request-scoped state instead of throwing not-implemented errors.
details: The adapter now loads a runtime-state snapshot from Postgres and commits back through a versioned row.
Existing repository behavior is reused against that request-scoped state, which keeps the product logic aligned between memory and Postgres modes.
A dedicated migration now creates the runtime-state table required by the adapter.

evidence.note: Postgres adapter now loads persisted runtime state.
Postgres adapter now commits runtime state back to Postgres.
Adapter targets the runtime-state table instead of throwing not-implemented errors.
A dedicated migration now creates the runtime-state table.

readyFoundations.title: Database migration tooling
readyFoundations.summary: The repo now has a concrete migration workflow for provisioning the Postgres schema instead of leaving database bring-up as a manual, undocumented step.
details: Root commands now expose database status and migration execution.
The DB package tracks applied migrations in opsui_schema_migrations.
This turns DATABASE_URL activation into an operational procedure instead of an ad hoc task.

evidence.note: Root workspace now exposes a database migration command.
Root workspace now exposes a database status command.
Database migrations are tracked in a schema-migrations table.
DB status command reports applied vs pending migrations.

readyFoundations.title: OIDC provider boundary
readyFoundations.summary: The auth worker now exposes provider-aware login, callback, and logout routes, with generic OIDC token and userinfo exchange scaffolding instead of only a local mock-session lane.
details: The auth surface now has explicit OIDC login and callback routes.
Callback handling exchanges an authorization code for tokens and derives a session from userinfo claims.
Workspace mapping is still scaffolded, but the provider boundary is no longer missing.

evidence.note: Auth worker exposes an OIDC login route.
Auth worker exposes an OIDC callback route.
OIDC callback performs an authorization-code exchange.
Auth worker now carries OIDC configuration variables.

readyFoundations.title: Configurable OIDC workspace resolution
readyFoundations.summary: OIDC callback handling can now derive the workspace from a configured claim or email-domain map instead of always forcing every authenticated user into the default workspace.
details: Workspace resolution can come from an explicit OIDC claim.
A domain-to-workspace map can be used when claim-based routing is unavailable.
This reduces tenant coupling, even though full membership and role enforcement are still not complete.

evidence.note: OIDC callback now resolves workspace through a helper instead of hardcoding the default workspace.
Workspace resolution can use a configured OIDC claim.
Workspace resolution can fall back to an email-domain map.

readyFoundations.title: OIDC workspace and role guardrails
readyFoundations.summary: OIDC callback handling now fails closed for disallowed workspaces and carries a mapped workspace role into the signed session instead of treating every authenticated user as equally trusted.
details: Workspace access can now be restricted to an explicit allowlist.
A role claim or default role can be normalized into the session actor.
This is still not a substitute for database-backed membership enforcement, but it is much closer to a real production boundary.

evidence.note: OIDC callback now checks an explicit workspace allowlist.
OIDC callback now maps a workspace role into the session actor.
Disallowed workspace resolution now fails closed.
Auth worker config exposes allowed workspace ids.

readyFoundations.title: Realtime signaling and room-state primitives
readyFoundations.summary: The realtime worker now supports direct offer/answer/ICE relay plus room snapshot propagation, which moves it beyond a pure hand-raise presence channel.
details: Participants can relay WebRTC signaling messages through the Durable Object.
Room snapshots now include lock and recording state and can be requested or broadcast on change.
This is still not a full production media plane, but it is a more credible realtime control foundation.

evidence.note: Realtime coordinator can relay offer messages.
Realtime coordinator can relay answer messages.
Realtime coordinator can relay ICE candidates.
Realtime coordinator can publish room snapshots.

readyFoundations.title: Realtime backend control sync
readyFoundations.summary: The realtime worker can now accept backend room-state patches and the API pushes join, moderation, recording, and lifecycle updates into it, so websocket room state no longer drifts as easily from backend truth.
details: The Durable Object now exposes an internal state-sync route in addition to websocket handling.
API room lifecycle routes now best-effort sync lock, lobby, admit, remove, recording, and end-meeting state into realtime.
Realtime health now reports control-sync readiness separately from raw websocket availability.

evidence.note: Realtime coordinator now accepts internal state-sync requests.
Realtime coordinator can merge backend room-state patches into the live snapshot.
API worker now has a helper for syncing backend state into realtime.
Realtime health now reports control-sync readiness.

readyFoundations.title: Edge rate-limit baseline
readyFoundations.summary: The public auth and API edges now have explicit request throttles for join, mock session, and hook-trigger surfaces instead of relying on no abuse controls at all.
details: Join-token and mock-session issuance are rate limited in the auth worker.
Meeting join and hook-trigger routes are rate limited in the API worker.
This is still a local fixed-window baseline, but it closes the previous zero-control gap.

evidence.note: Auth worker now exposes a shared rate-limit helper.
Auth join-token issuance is rate limited.
API worker now exposes a shared rate-limit helper.
Meeting join is rate limited.

readyFoundations.title: Preview smoke runner
readyFoundations.summary: The repo now includes an env-driven smoke runner for deployed preview surfaces, so ops can validate bound hostnames and published artifacts without inventing one-off checks.
details: Worker surfaces are checked through their health endpoints.
Docs and preview Pages surfaces are checked for both page response and published topology/readiness assets.
The runner is optional until preview host URLs are configured, which keeps local verification stable while still allowing CI execution.

evidence.note: Root package now exposes a preview smoke script.
Preview smoke runner uses env-configured targets.
Preview smoke runner verifies published readiness assets.
CI can execute preview smoke checks when preview URLs are configured.

prelaunchHardening.title: Deployed preview smoke execution still needs rollout wiring
prelaunchHardening.summary: The smoke runner now exists, but deployed preview host URLs and regular execution still need to be wired into an environment where those domains are actually live.
details: Local and CI verification now support deployed preview smoke execution when preview URLs are configured.
Launch readiness still needs those preview URLs to be provisioned and maintained against live preview domains.

evidence.note: Preview smoke runner now exists in the root package.
CI can run preview smoke checks, but only after preview URLs are configured.

prelaunchHardening.title: External alerting and SLO rollout still need first-class work
prelaunchHardening.summary: Worker telemetry and in-product audit visibility now exist, but production launch still needs alert routing, thresholds, and SLO ownership outside the product UI.
details: The product already exposes useful admin and room activity surfaces, and the Workers now emit baseline analytics datapoints.
Production readiness still needs external alert routing, thresholding, dashboards, and on-call ownership outside the app surfaces.

evidence.note: Auth worker telemetry baseline now exists.
API worker telemetry baseline now exists.
Admin UI already exposes failure triage.
Admin audit view exists, but external monitoring is still separate work.
